CoachingSession.title: CoachingSession
CoachingSession.description: Represents a single real-time AI coaching session between a user and the AI coach, tracking its lifecycle and key data points for historical and analytical purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CoachingSession entity.

userId.description: Identifier for the user who initiated this session, as provided by the external authentication system. This links the session to a specific user. (Relationship: User 1:N CoachingSession)

startTime.description: The UTC timestamp when the coaching session officially began.

endTime.description: The UTC timestamp when the coaching session concluded. This property will be null for sessions that are currently active or were not completed.

status.description: The current lifecycle status of the coaching session (e.g., 'active', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'failed').

durationSeconds.description: The total duration of the coaching session in seconds, calculated upon its completion.

simliSessionId.description: Optional unique identifier provided by the Simli platform for its internal tracking of this specific session. Useful for debugging or linking to Simli's records.

geminiConversationId.description: Optional unique identifier for the Gemini Multimodal Live conversation associated with this session, useful for tracing interactions within the Gemini API.

transcript.description: The full text transcript of the coaching session's verbal exchanges, capturing all spoken dialogue between the user and the AI coach.

summary.description: An AI-generated summary or key takeaways from the coaching session, typically provided upon its completion to highlight important points or actions.

definition.description: Stores individual coaching sessions for each user. Each session document includes a denormalized 'userId' field, which matches the '{userId}' in the path for robust authorization independence and simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this coaching session.

params.name: coachingSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific coaching session.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering strictly to the core design principles. The central entity, `CoachingSession`, is designed to be tightly coupled with its owner, the user.

**1. Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Each `CoachingSession` document will be stored within a user-specific subcollection: `users/{userId}/coachingSessions/{coachingSessionId}`. The `CoachingSession` schema explicitly includes a `userId` field. This denormalizes the ownership information directly into the document itself, making it redundant with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This redundancy is crucial. It means security rules can directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the path wildcard) without needing to perform any `get()` operations to check parent documents or other entities. This completely eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations (transactions/batches) and significantly simplifying rule logic. For example, a user can create a `CoachingSession` document in their own subcollection without any complex checks on a parent `User` document.

**2. QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**
This hierarchical structure is fundamental for implementing Queryable Authorization Policies (QAPs). By enforcing that `CoachingSession` documents live under `users/{userId}/coachingSessions`, a user can only perform `list` operations against their *own* subcollection (i.e., `users/{request.auth.uid}/coachingSessions`). Security rules will ensure that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path for any read access. This naturally filters results by owner at the path level, ensuring that rules are not used as filters and preventing over-fetching or unauthorized data exposure. All documents within `users/{userId}/coachingSessions` share the identical security posture (owned by `{userId}`), achieving **Structural Segregation** and simplifying rules to a single, clear ownership check. There's no mix of public and private data within this specific collection.

**3. Clarity of Intent and Debuggability:**
 The path `users/{userId}/coachingSessions/{coachingSessionId}` explicitly communicates the ownership of each session. The inclusion of an explicit `status` field (e.g., 'active', 'completed') within the `CoachingSession` document adheres to the **Explicit State Modeling** principle, making the lifecycle of a session clear and predictable, which greatly assists in understanding and debugging both application logic and security rules. Wildcards (`{userId}`, `{coachingSessionId}`) are descriptive, enhancing readability.

In summary, this design leverages path-based ownership and intentional denormalization of the `userId` to create an inherently secure, performant, and easy-to-manage Firestore structure for coaching sessions, fully aligning with all mandated design principles.
